items.headline: City Council reviews capital improvement spending plans for next fiscal year
items.summary: The council held a study session on April 14 to discuss the Fiscal Year 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program, which outlines major infrastructure and development projects the city plans to fund. This meeting determines what roads, parks, buildings, and other public assets will be prioritized and funded in the coming year.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance amending the Residential Tenant Protections Program. This could affect renter rights and landlord obligations in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: Library releases new facilities and customer experience improvement plan
items.summary: San José Library presented a status report on its Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9, 2026. The plan outlines improvements to library branches and services for residents.

items.headline: Downtown historic building gets density bonus for renovation
items.summary: The property at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto is being elevated from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status, qualifying it for an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area in exchange for seismic and historic rehabilitation work. This could reshape the building's future use and size.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A proposal to rezone and redevelop two parcels at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road would bring an 8-story building with 167 residential units (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents significant densification of the San Antonio Road corridor and was under public hearing review.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing delayed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled on April 8, 2026, with no new hearing date set yet. Residents interested in this development will need to watch for future notices when the hearing is rescheduled.

items.headline: Grant awarded for Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding will help expand cultural programming and arts education at the community venue.

items.headline: City approves major local streets pavement repair project for 2026
items.summary: The City Council awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project, which will address deteriorated street conditions across San José. This is part of the city's ongoing infrastructure maintenance program.
